meaning: adj.未获报答(或报偿、报酬、奖赏)的
examples: He added that the King his father would not let her loyal kindness and devotion go unrewarded.
Talent will not: Nothing is more common than unrewarded talent.
Discounting the positive makes you feel inadequate and unrewarded.
However, their pressure went unrewarded after Paterson saw a penalty rebound clear off the crossbar.
This is another illustration of the inside-the-Beltway adage that no bad deed goes unrewarded.
